mappings.notes: Sensitive data in labelling tasks accessed by adversary-controlled or compromised labelling vendor
Labelling vendor's data stores containing sensitive annotation tasks accessed as information repositories
Adversary-controlled labelling vendor introduces biased or poisoned annotations into training data
HITL data governance policy — data minimisation, contractor handling requirements, retention limits
All labelling vendors and HITL providers mapped — data access scope, contractual protections
Privacy assessment of labelling workflows — annotator data access scope, anonymisation effectiveness
Residual privacy risk from labelling vendor access documented and treated

mappings.control_id: Training data governance includes human annotation workflows
mappings.notes: Labeller data access controls and data minimisation are Art. 10 requirements for high-risk training data

mappings.control_id: Labelling vendors are part of the AI value chain — obligations distributed accordingly
mappings.notes: Labelling vendor security requirements and contractual controls are Art. 25 value chain obligations
Data minimisation and privacy controls for all HITL labelling workflows
Security training for all labellers — internal and third-party — covering data handling requirements
Contractual data handling requirements for labelling vendors and HITL service providers
Anonymisation and redaction applied to labelling tasks before exposure to annotators

mappings.control_name: V8.3.3 — Consent for personal data processing

mappings.notes: Labelling vendors with OT data access assessed under 62443-2-4 — same requirements as OT software vendors
OT data minimisation in labelling tasks — annotators see minimum content needed, not full process records
All labelling vendors with OT data access assessed before engagement
OT GenAI pipeline failure as availability risk
Cascade failure scenarios in OT risk register
Circuit breakers between OT data pipeline layers
Labelling vendors treated as suppliers — data handling requirements in contracts, compliance monitored
Data minimisation — annotators access minimum content required for annotation function
Acceptable use policy covers labelling activities — what data may be used in labelling workflows
Labelling staff trained on data handling — classification, prohibited use, incident reporting

mappings.control_id: Labelling vendors assessed in vendor risk programme — data access scope, handling requirements, sub-processor chain
Contractual data handling obligations for labelling vendors — data minimisation, retention, permitted use
Personal information accessed by labellers documented — purpose, scope, and safeguards
Labelling vendor use of personal information restricted — no secondary use, training, or retention beyond task

mappings.notes: Labelling vendors with access to CHD are TPSPs — add to TPSP list, initiate compliance process
Written agreements with labelling vendors acknowledging CHD security responsibility
SAD must never appear in labelling tasks — labels cannot expose CVV or full magnetic stripe data
Labelling task data minimised — annotators see minimum CHD required for annotation function
Data labeling and human review services governed as AI supply chain components — DPA, access controls, and data handling requirements contractually mandated
Data provided to human reviewers classified and minimised — no unnecessary sensitive data in labeling batches
All human reviewer data access logged — access scope, duration, and data volume tracked
Labeling service providers assessed as supply chain components — security controls, sub-contractor restrictions, jurisdiction reviewed
Design circuit breakers and isolation boundaries between data pipeline stages
Detect correlated failures across pipeline stages; alert before full cascade
Continuous health checks across all pipeline components
Verify blast radius containment design before deployment
Documented runbook for data pipeline cascade scenarios

mappings.control_id: Shared credential across pipeline stages — one failure affects all
mappings.notes: Separate credentials per pipeline stage

mappings.control_id: Single credential covering multiple pipeline stages amplifies cascade
mappings.notes: Per-stage minimum scope credentials

mappings.control_name: Reuse existing well-secured software — third-party data vetting
mappings.notes: Vet all third-party data sources, APIs, and pre-trained components for security, quality, compliance, and provenance before use in AI pipelines

mappings.control_name: Verify software integrity — third-party data integrity
mappings.notes: Verify integrity of all third-party data deliveries using checksums, signatures, or schema validation; detect tampering or corruption before pipeline ingestion

mappings.control_name: Identify and confirm vulnerabilities — third-party monitoring
mappings.notes: Monitor for security advisories and quality issues from third-party data providers; establish triage procedures for third-party data incidents

mappings.control_name: External Information System Services — third-party data controls
mappings.notes: Require FedRAMP authorisation for third-party data service providers; establish SLAs covering data security, privacy, and incident notification

mappings.control_name: Supply Chain Risk Management Plan — data provider risk
mappings.notes: Include third-party data providers in supply chain risk management; assess data handling practices and security posture

mappings.control_name: Supply Chain Controls — data provenance from third parties
mappings.notes: Verify provenance and integrity of data from third-party sources; implement validation before ingestion into AI pipelines

mappings.control_name: Third-Party Risk — data provider and processor oversight
mappings.notes: Include all third-party data providers and processors in DORA third-party risk management; conduct due diligence, establish contracts, and monitor ongoing compliance
Register all third-party data sources in ICT asset inventory; document provenance, contracts, and dependency relationships
Participate in information sharing arrangements for third-party AI data risks; share threat intelligence on data provider compromises

incidents.name: Scale AI / Sama contractor data exposure — third-party AI labeling workforce privacy violations
